info.title: Bluesky HTTP API Reference — Bluesky App
info.summary: HTTP/XRPC endpoint reference for Bluesky and AT Protocol lexicons.
info.description:
This is the HTTP API reference for Bluesky. It covers the `app.bsky.*`, `com.atproto.*`, `chat.bsky.*`, and `tools.ozone.*` Lexicon namespaces — the Lexicons implemented by the Bluesky application, its related services (DMs, Ozone moderation), and the AT Protocol [PDS](https://atproto.com/guides/the-at-stack#pds) those services build on. Use the document switcher in the top left to move between endpoints.

The wider AT Protocol Lexicon ecosystem is open and any service can publish its own Lexicons. For an index of community Lexicons see [lexicon.garden](https://lexicon.garden/), and for the schema language itself see the [Lexicon guide](https://atproto.com/guides/lexicon).

## Authentication and request routing

These endpoints don't all live on the same host, and where a request should be sent depends on whether the caller is authenticated:

- **Most `app.bsky.*` `GET`s are public** and can be called without authentication against the Bluesky AppView at `https://public.api.bsky.app`. `POST`s (writes) and any endpoint that returns account-private data require auth.
- **Authenticated requests should be sent to the user's own PDS**. The PDS validates the session and, if needed, proxies the request to the correct backend. Proxied requests should [include an `atproto-proxy` header](https://atproto.com/specs/xrpc#service-proxying). [Bluesky DMs](#bluesky-dms/description/introduction) and [Ozone Moderation](#ozone-moderation/description/introduction) requests always require proxying.

For client libraries that handle session management and proxying for you, see the [AT Protocol SDKs](https://atproto.com/sdks).

servers.description: Public Bluesky AppView. Use this for unauthenticated `app.bsky.*` reads — no token required.

Provide your PDS hostname. Use `bsky.social` if your account is hosted by Bluesky; replace it with your own PDS hostname (e.g. `pds.example.com`) if you're self-hosted. The PDS handles auth and proxies `app.bsky` / `chat.bsky` / `tools.ozone` calls onward. To get a token to make requests from this page, call `com.atproto.server.createSession` with your handle and an [app password](https://bsky.app/settings/app-passwords), and paste the returned `accessJwt` into the **Authentication** panel below. The token is then attached to every test request automatically.

get.description: Get private preferences attached to the current account. Expected use is synchronization between multiple devices, and import/export during account migration. Requires auth.

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript (@atproto/lex)
x-codeSamples.source:
import * as app from './lexicons/app.js'

const result = await client.call(app.bsky.actor.getPreferences)

x-codeSamples.label: Go (indigo)
Shell (curl)
x-codeSamples.source:
curl 'https://bsky.social/xrpc/app.bsky.actor.getPreferences' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>'

get.description: Find actors (profiles) matching search criteria. Does not require auth.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query string. Syntax, phrase, boolean, and faceting is unspecified, but Lucene query syntax is recommended.

schema.description: Search query string. Syntax, phrase, boolean, and faceting is unspecified, but Lucene query syntax is recommended.

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript (@atproto/lex)
x-codeSamples.source:
import * as app from './lexicons/app.js'

const result = await client.call(app.bsky.actor.searchActors, {
  q: '',
  term: '',
  limit: 25,
  cursor: '',
})

x-codeSamples.label: Go (indigo)
x-codeSamples.source:
import (
	"context"
	appbsky "github.com/bluesky-social/indigo/api/bsky"
)

ctx := context.Background()

out, err := appbsky.ActorSearchActors(ctx, client,
	"", // cursor
	25, // limit
	"", // q
	"", // term
)

x-codeSamples.label: Shell (curl)
x-codeSamples.source:
curl 'https://bsky.social/xrpc/app.bsky.actor.searchActors?q=&term=&limit=25&cursor=' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>'

post.description: Initiate Age Assurance for an account.

email.description: The user's email address to receive Age Assurance instructions.

language.description: The user's preferred language for communication during the Age Assurance process.

regionCode.description: An optional ISO 3166-2 code of the user's region or state within the country.

countryCode.description: An ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the user's location.

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript (@atproto/lex)

import * as app from './lexicons/app.js'

const result = await client.call(app.bsky.ageassurance.begin, {
  email: '',

  countryCode: '',
})

  "email": "",

  "regionCode": "",

get.description: Find posts matching search criteria, returning views of those posts. Note that this API endpoint may require authentication (eg, not public) for some service providers and implementations.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query string; syntax, phrase, boolean, and faceting is unspecified, but Lucene query syntax is recommended.

schema.description: Search query string; syntax, phrase, boolean, and faceting is unspecified, but Lucene query syntax is recommended.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Filter to posts with the given tag (hashtag), based on rich-text facet or tag field. Do not include the hash (#) prefix. Multiple tags can be specified, with 'AND' matching.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Filter to posts with links (facet links or embeds) pointing to this URL. Server may apply URL normalization or fuzzy matching.

schema.description: Filter to posts with links (facet links or embeds) pointing to this URL. Server may apply URL normalization or fuzzy matching.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Filter to posts in the given language. Expected to be based on post language field, though server may override language detection.

schema.description: Filter to posts in the given language. Expected to be based on post language field, though server may override language detection.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Specifies the ranking order of results.

schema.description: Specifies the ranking order of results.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Filter results for posts after the indicated datetime (inclusive). Expected to use 'sortAt' timestamp, which may not match 'createdAt'. Can be a datetime, or just an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD).

schema.description: Filter results for posts after the indicated datetime (inclusive). Expected to use 'sortAt' timestamp, which may not match 'createdAt'. Can be a datetime, or just an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Filter results for posts before the indicated datetime (not inclusive). Expected to use 'sortAt' timestamp, which may not match 'createdAt'. Can be a datetime, or just an ISO date (YYY-MM-DD).

schema.description: Filter results for posts before the indicated datetime (not inclusive). Expected to use 'sortAt' timestamp, which may not match 'createdAt'. Can be a datetime, or just an ISO date (YYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: author
parameters.description: Filter to posts by the given account. Handles are resolved to DID before query-time.

schema.description: Filter to posts by the given account. Handles are resolved to DID before query-time.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Optional pagination mechanism; may not necessarily allow scrolling through entire result set.

schema.description: Optional pagination mechanism; may not necessarily allow scrolling through entire result set.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Filter to posts with URLs (facet links or embeds) linking to the given domain (hostname). Server may apply hostname normalization.

schema.description: Filter to posts with URLs (facet links or embeds) linking to the given domain (hostname). Server may apply hostname normalization.

parameters.name: mentions
parameters.description: Filter to posts which mention the given account. Handles are resolved to DID before query-time. Only matches rich-text facet mentions.

schema.description: Filter to posts which mention the given account. Handles are resolved to DID before query-time. Only matches rich-text facet mentions.

app.bsky.graph.getStarterPacksWithMember.description: A starter pack and an optional list item indicating membership of a target user to that starter pack.

app.bsky.notification.defs.subjectActivi.description: Object used to store activity subscription data in stash.

reason.description: The reason why this notification was delivered - e.g. your post was liked, or you received a new follower.

app.bsky.richtext.facet.tag.description: Facet feature for a hashtag. The text usually includes a '#' prefix, but the facet reference should not (except in the case of 'double hash tags').

app.bsky.richtext.facet.link.description: Facet feature for a URL. The text URL may have been simplified or truncated, but the facet reference should be a complete URL.

app.bsky.richtext.facet.description: Annotation of a sub-string within rich text.

app.bsky.richtext.facet.mention.description: Facet feature for mention of another account. The text is usually a handle, including a '@' prefix, but the facet reference is a DID.

app.bsky.richtext.facet.byteSlice.description: Specifies the sub-string range a facet feature applies to. Start index is inclusive, end index is exclusive. Indices are zero-indexed, counting bytes of the UTF-8 encoded text. NOTE: some languages, like Javascript, use UTF-16 or Unicode codepoints for string slice indexing; in these languages, convert to byte arrays before working with facets.

state.description: The state of the video processing job. All values not listed as a known value indicate that the job is in process.

chat.bsky.actor.defs.groupConvoMember.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here].

chatDisabled.description: Set to true when the actor cannot actively participate in conversations

chat.bsky.actor.defs.directConvoMember.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here].

chat.bsky.convo.defs.groupConvo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here].

name.description: The display name of the group conversation.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.directConvo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here].

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logAddMember.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a member was added to a group convo. The member who was added gets a logBeginConvo (to create the convo) but also a logAddMember (to show the system message as the first message the user sees).

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logEditGroup.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating info about group convo was edited.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logLockConvo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a group convo was locked.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logMuteConvo.description: Event indicating the viewer muted a convo. Can be direct or group.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logReadConvo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a convo was read up to a certain message.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logBeginConvo.description: Event indicating a convo containing the viewer was started. Can be direct or group. When a member is added to a group convo, they also get this event.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logLeaveConvo.description: Event indicating the viewer left a convo. Can be direct or group.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logMemberJoin.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a member joined a group convo via join link. The member who was added gets a logBeginConvo (to create the convo) but also a logMemberJoin (to show the system message as the first message the user sees).

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logAcceptConvo.description: Event indicating the viewer accepted a convo, and it can be moved out of the request inbox. Can be direct or group.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logAddReaction.description: Event indicating a reaction was added to a message.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logMemberLeave.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a member voluntarily left a group convo. The member who was removed gets a logLeaveConvo (to leave the convo) but not a logMemberLeave (because they already left, so can't see the system message).

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logReadMessage.description: DEPRECATED: use logReadConvo instead. Event indicating a convo was read up to a certain message.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logUnlockConvo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a group convo was unlocked.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logUnmuteConvo.description: Event indicating the viewer unmuted a convo. Can be direct or group.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logEditJoinLink.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a settings about a join link for a group convo were edited.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logRemoveMember.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a member was removed from a group convo. The member who was removed gets a logLeaveConvo (to leave the convo) but not a logRemoveMember (because they already left, so can't see the system message).

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logCreateMessage.description: Event indicating a user-originated message was created. Is not emitted for system messages.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logDeleteMessage.description: Event indicating a user-originated message was deleted. Is not emitted for system messages.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logCreateJoinLink.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join link was created for a group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logEnableJoinLink.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join link was enabled for a group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logRemoveReaction.description: Event indicating a reaction was removed from a message.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageView.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here].

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logDisableJoinLink.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join link was disabled for a group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logRejectJoinReques.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join request was rejected by the viewer. Only the owner gets this.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logApproveJoinReque.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join request was approved by the viewer. Only the owner gets this. The approved member gets a logBeginConvo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logIncomingJoinRequ.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join request was made to a group the viewer owns. Only the owner gets this.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logOutgoingJoinRequ.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a join request was made by the viewer.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.logLockConvoPermane.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. Event indicating a group convo was locked permanently.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataAd.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating a user was added to the group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataEd.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group info was edited.

newName.description: Group name that replaced the old.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataLo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group convo was locked.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataMe.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating a user joined the group convo via join link.
[NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating a user voluntarily left the group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataUn.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group convo was unlocked.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataEd.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group join link was edited.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataRe.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating a user was removed from the group convo.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataCr.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group join link was created.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataEn.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group join link was enabled.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataDi.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group join link was disabled.

chat.bsky.convo.defs.systemMessageDataLo.description: [NOTE: This is under active development and should be considered unstable while this note is here]. System message indicating the group convo was locked permanently.

handle.description: The validated handle of the account; or 'handle.invalid' if the handle did not bi-directionally match the DID document.

com.atproto.label.defs.label.description: Metadata tag on an atproto resource (eg, repo or record).

cid.description: Optionally, CID specifying the specific version of 'uri' resource this label applies to.

cts.description: Timestamp when this label was created.

exp.description: Timestamp at which this label expires (no longer applies).

neg.description: If true, this is a negation label, overwriting a previous label.

src.description: DID of the actor who created this label.

uri.description: AT URI of the record, repository (account), or other resource that this label applies to.

val.description: The short string name of the value or type of this label.

com.atproto.label.defs.selfLabel.description: Metadata tag on an atproto record, published by the author within the record. Note that schemas should use #selfLabels, not #selfLabel.

val.description: The short string name of the value or type of this label.

com.atproto.label.defs.selfLabels.description: Metadata tags on an atproto record, published by the author within the record.

com.atproto.label.defs.labelValueDefinit.description: Declares a label value and its expected interpretations and behaviors.

blurs.description: What should this label hide in the UI, if applied? 'content' hides all of the target; 'media' hides the images/video/audio; 'none' hides nothing.

severity.description: How should a client visually convey this label? 'inform' means neutral and informational; 'alert' means negative and warning; 'none' means show nothing.

adultOnly.description: Does the user need to have adult content enabled in order to configure this label?

identifier.description: The value of the label being defined. Must only include lowercase ascii and the '-' character ([a-z-]+).

defaultSetting.description: The default setting for this label.

com.atproto.label.defs.labelValueDefinit.description: Strings which describe the label in the UI, localized into a specific language.

lang.description: The code of the language these strings are written in.

name.description: A short human-readable name for the label.

description.description: A longer description of what the label means and why it might be applied.

com.atproto.moderation.defs.reasonViolat.description: Direct violation of server rules, laws, terms of service. Prefer new lexicon definition `tools.ozone.report.defs#reasonRuleOther`.

com.atproto.moderation.defs.reasonMislea.description: Misleading identity, affiliation, or content. Prefer new lexicon definition `tools.ozone.report.defs#reasonMisleadingOther`.

hostname.description: hostname of server; not a URL (no scheme)
